But if you meant "Thymus serpyllum" or "Thyme" (Thymbra or Thymus)  then the following information is about Thyme:

Thyme is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial plant (Thymus spp.) native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and purple flowers. Thyme has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in soothing respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and supporting overall immune function. The bioactive constituents of thyme include thymol, carvacrol, linalool, and rosmarinic acid, which contribute to its medicinal and aromatic properties. Thyme is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, essential oils, and topical creams, often used to alleviate respiratory issues, boost immunity, and promote wound healing.
